skill.name: huawei-cloud-modelarts-skill
skill.description: Manage Huawei Cloud ModelArts resources, training jobs, models, inference services, and notebooks through ModelArts SDK actions.
skill.summary: Operate common Huawei Cloud ModelArts resources from an AI assistant workflow.
skill.author: modelarts-agent

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported critical risk, but review found no prompt injection, obfuscation, weak cryptography, or credential exfiltration evidence. The confirmed risks are legitimate cloud API operations, a shell initializer, documentation commands, and the ability to create ModelArts training jobs with caller-supplied parameters.

medium_findings.title: Cloud Training Job Creation Requires Privileged Context
medium_findings.description: The skill can create ModelArts training jobs using caller-provided code_dir, boot_file, job_name, and flavor values. This is a legitimate ModelArts capability, but it can consume cloud resources and run selected training code under the active ModelArts session.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The function constructs an Estimator from request parameters and calls fit with wait=False. The behavior is explicit and useful, but it has cost and execution impact in the target cloud account.

medium_findings.title: Cloud Resource Enumeration Through ModelArts SDK
medium_findings.description: The skill lists OBS buckets, training jobs, models, services, and notebooks through the ModelArts SDK. This is expected for a resource management skill, but it exposes cloud inventory metadata to the assistant response path.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The SDK calls are directly visible and are part of the documented purpose. The risk depends on caller permissions and response handling rather than hidden malicious behavior.

medium_findings.title: Exception Message May Leak Operational Details
medium_findings.description: The resource overview action returns str(e) directly on failure. SDK exceptions may include endpoint, project, region, or request context details that should normally be normalized before returning to users.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The direct exception string is present in one action. I did not find evidence of credential output, but cloud SDK errors can include operational metadata.

low_findings.title: Documentation Contains Shell Commands and External URLs
low_findings.description: README.md includes clone, install, git workflow commands, and links to Huawei Cloud documentation. These are documentation examples, not runtime command execution by the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands and URLs appear inside Markdown instructions. They are not invoked by application code, so the static external command and hardcoded URL findings are mostly false positives.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Findings Were Not Confirmed
low_findings.description: The files flagged for weak cryptography do not show hashing, encryption, or weak algorithm calls in the reviewed locations. The tools/security.py code performs recursive redaction of sensitive field names.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: No MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or similar algorithm usage appears in the cited code. The static hits appear to be keyword-based false positives.

low_findings.title: Entropy and Dangerous Combination Heuristics Were Not Confirmed
low_findings.description: README.md contains normal Chinese documentation and Markdown examples. I did not find encoded payloads, binary content, credential exfiltration, or prompt injection instructions in the reviewed files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found readable project documentation rather than obfuscated content. The code has cloud SDK access, but not the malicious combination implied by the heuristic.

dangerous_patterns.title: Privileged Cloud API Actions
dangerous_patterns.description: The action map exposes list and create operations for ModelArts resources. Operators should run it with least-privilege temporary credentials and require confirmation for job creation.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The exposed actions are explicit and consistent with the skill description. The security concern is account impact if credentials are too broad.

content.value_statement: ModelArts users need quick visibility into training, models, services, and notebooks. This skill provides assistant-friendly actions for common ModelArts resource management tasks.
actual_capabilities: List a basic OBS-backed resource overview through a ModelArts session.
List ModelArts training jobs with a configurable result limit.
Create a ModelArts training job from provided code and boot file parameters.
List registered ModelArts models through the SDK.
List inference services and notebooks through ModelArts SDK calls.
Redact common credential, network, and token field names in returned data.
limitations: Requires a configured Huawei Cloud ModelArts runtime and valid temporary credentials.
Does not include approval gates before creating training jobs.
Does not validate all job parameters before sending them to ModelArts.
Some cloud SDK errors may be returned as generic failure messages.

use_cases.title: Check Active AI Resources
use_cases.description: Review available ModelArts resources before starting operational work.

use_cases.title: Monitor Model Development Work
use_cases.description: List training jobs, models, notebooks, and inference services from one assistant workflow.

use_cases.title: Start Standard Training Runs
use_cases.description: Create a ModelArts training job using approved code locations and runtime settings.

prompt_templates.title: List Resource Overview
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner check of connected ModelArts resources.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Huawei Cloud ModelArts skill to show my current resource overview. Do not expose credentials or network identifiers.

prompt_templates.title: Review Training Jobs
prompt_templates.prompt: List my latest ModelArts training jobs with a limit of five. Summarize status, names, and any redacted fields.

prompt_templates.title: Inspect Deployment Assets
prompt_templates.scenario: Check models and inference services before a release.
prompt_templates.prompt: List available ModelArts models and inference services. Highlight resources that may need deployment review.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare Training Job Request
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a ModelArts training job only after confirming the job_name, code_dir, boot_file, and flavor values I provide.

output_examples.output: The skill returns a successful resource overview with the OBS bucket count and a confirmation message. Sensitive fields are redacted when present.

output_examples.input: List the latest five training jobs.
output_examples.output: The skill returns recent training job records from ModelArts with credential, token, endpoint, VPC, and security group values masked.

output_examples.input: Create a training job for an approved code directory.
output_examples.output: The skill submits a ModelArts training job and returns the created job details after applying redaction to sensitive fields.

best_practices: Use temporary credentials with the minimum ModelArts permissions needed for each task.
Confirm job creation parameters before invoking the create training job action.
Review returned cloud inventory data before sharing it outside the trusted workspace.
anti_patterns: Do not run the skill with broad administrator credentials for routine checks.
Do not pass unreviewed code directories or boot files into training job creation.
Do not rely on the redaction helper as the only control for sensitive data handling.

faq.question: What does this skill manage?
faq.answer: It manages common Huawei Cloud ModelArts resources, including training jobs, models, inference services, notebooks, and a basic resource overview.

faq.question: Does it store Huawei Cloud credentials?
faq.answer: No storage is shown in the reviewed code. It creates a ModelArts Session from the runtime environment.

faq.question: Can it create cloud resources?
faq.answer: Yes. It can create ModelArts training jobs, so operators should require confirmation and least-privilege credentials.

faq.question: Does it make network requests?
faq.answer: Yes. The ModelArts SDK actions communicate with Huawei Cloud services.

faq.question: Is sensitive data redacted?
faq.answer: The helper redacts common credential, token, endpoint, subnet, VPC, and security group field names.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: It is publishable with a medium risk warning because cloud job creation and resource listing require careful permissions.
